questions: formal writing ar academic writing ki same?
academic writing e ki ki avoid korbo?
answers: Academic writing is formal, evidence-based writing used in scholarly contexts. It requires clear argumentation, proper citations, objective tone, and follows specific formatting guidelines like APA or MLA.
Academic writing holo scholarly lekha ja research, analysis, ar evidence er upor based. Formal tone, third person, ar proper referencing must. Informal language ar slang avoid koro.
Good academic writing is clear, concise, well-structured, and properly cited. Use topic sentences, evidence to support claims, logical transitions, and always revise your drafts multiple times.

Academic writing requires objectivity, precision, and adherence to citation standards. Avoid first person (unless required), use hedging language for claims, and support all arguments with credible sources.
